title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer about Machiavelli, arguing he is widely misunderstood. Palmer explores Machiavelli's career as a Florentine diplomat, his torture and exile by the Medici, and how his political insights, compiled into his famous work, were withheld from other powers to protect Florence, analogous to Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer, who argues that Machiavelli is widely misread. The conversation covers his career as a Florentine diplomat, his torture and exile under the Medici, and the political insights he compiled into his famous work. Palmer frames those insights as having been withheld from rival powers to protect Florence, drawing an analogy to Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR.
